training_data.description: 2,000 UK customer reviews modelled on Trustpilot and Amazon UK review patterns

split.strategy: Stratified split preserving class proportions in all three sets

architecture.base: DistilBERT — distilled version of BERT, 40% smaller and 60% faster

training_config.class_weights: Computed on train set only via sklearn compute_class_weight balanced
training_config.early_stopping: Patience 2 on validation loss — best checkpoint saved

performance.evaluation_note: All metrics reported on held-out test set evaluated exactly once after training

intended_use.primary_use: Automated sentiment triage for UK e-commerce and retail customer reviews
use_cases: Flagging negative reviews for customer service teams
Tracking sentiment trends over time by product category
Prioritising complaint queues by sentiment confidence
not_intended_for: Medical, legal, or financial decision making
Non-English or non-UK dialect reviews without further fine-tuning
Real-time production systems without additional safety evaluation
Reviews shorter than 5 words where confidence will be low

known_limitations: Neutral class has lowest F1 score (72.2%) due to inherent semantic ambiguity and being the smallest class (250 samples)
Trained exclusively on English UK retail reviews — performance may degrade on other dialects, domains, or languages
Very short reviews (fewer than 5 words) produce lower-confidence outputs and should be flagged for manual review
Sarcasm and irony are not reliably detected — for example 'Oh great, another broken product' may be misclassified as positive
Priority complaint flagging uses rule-based keyword matching — novel phrasings of safety or fraud complaints may be missed
Class imbalance (positive 52.8%, negative 34.7%, neutral 12.5%) means the model may be slightly biased toward positive predictions on ambiguous text

bias_and_fairness.demographic_bias_evaluation: Not performed on this version — recommended before production deployment
bias_and_fairness.geographic_bias: Trained on UK English only — regional dialect differences not evaluated
bias_and_fairness.category_bias: Electronics category has most samples and may have strongest performance
bias_and_fairness.recommendation: Evaluate per-category and per-company performance on real data before deployment

output_format.sentiment: string — one of: negative, neutral, positive
output_format.topics: list of strings — rule-based topic labels

priority.tier: string — Critical, High, Watch, or None
priority.signals: list of strings — priority categories detected
